examples.title: POSIX Pthreads Creation and Join
examples.description: Demonstrates basic thread creation using Pthreads library in C
examples.explanation: pthread_create() spawns a new thread. pthread_join() waits for thread termination. This demonstrates One-to-One model where each user thread maps to a kernel thread.

examples.title: Python Threading Example
examples.description: Demonstrates thread creation using Python's threading module
examples.code:
import threading
import time

def worker(thread_id):
    print(f"Thread {thread_id} starting")
    time.sleep(1)  # Simulate work
    print(f"Thread {thread_id} completed")

# Create and start threads
threads = []
for i in range(3):
    t = threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(i+1,))
    threads.append(t)
    t.start()

# Wait for all threads
for t in threads:
    t.join()

print("All threads finished")
examples.explanation: Python's threading module creates user-level threads (Many-to-One on CPython due to GIL). Threads run concurrently but GIL limits true parallel execution for CPU-bound tasks.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n threads to complete

examples.title: Round Robin Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Simulates Round Robin CPU scheduling with time quantum
examples.explanation: This simulates Round Robin where each process gets quantum time slice. Shows how waiting and turnaround times are calculated. Processes are cycled through until all complete.

examples.title: Java Thread Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates thread creation using Java's Thread class and Runnable interface
examples.explanation: Java uses One-to-One model where each Java thread maps to a native thread. Thread.start() creates new thread, Thread.join() waits for completion. Demonstrates platform-independent threading.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n threads to complete
